message:
fix: close SSH/TCP connections on all error paths and join stdin thread

Fixes resource leaks in the SFTP sync engine and the TUI asciicast
recorder.

src/sources/sync.rs — SSH/TCP cleanup:
- Clone TcpStream before handing ownership to ssh2::Session so we
  retain a handle for explicit shutdown
- Add a close_connections() helper that calls session.disconnect()
  and tcp.shutdown(Both) in order
- Call close_connections() on every early-return path: handshake
  failure, authentication failure, SFTP open failure, download failure,
  and normal completion
- Import std::net::Shutdown for the new calls
- Without this fix, failed SSH connections left TCP sockets in
  CLOSE_WAIT until the OS reaped them, which could exhaust file
  descriptors during bulk sync operations

src/tui_asciicast.rs — stdin forwarder thread:
- Store the stdin-forwarding thread's JoinHandle instead of detaching
- After the child process exits, join the thread if it has already
  finished (is_finished() check avoids blocking on a still-reading
  stdin)
- If the thread is still blocked on read(), intentionally drop the
  handle to detach—this is safe because the PTY writer is already
  closed
- Prevents a minor resource leak where the thread's stack was never
  reclaimed in the normal (non-blocking) exit path

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
